title: Mass. business confidence dips back into pessimism
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts business confidence index fell to 47 in March, down from 52 in February, amid rising energy costs and war in Iran.
Despite 178,000 new U.S. jobs in March, Massachusetts employers cited concerns over energy costs and geopolitical tensions affecting outlook.
The Associated Industries of Massachusetts notes steady economic growth, but warns of possible inflation impacts due to external factors.

title: For $6.5m, this Connecticut inn comes with a liquor license in trendy Mystic
author: Regina Cole
bullets: The Captain's Mansion in Mystic, Connecticut, is listed for $6.5 million as a mixed-use property.
The 10,335 sq ft Victorian inn includes eight bedrooms, a commercial kitchen, and a liquor license.
Located in Groton, the historic property features grand parlors, a dining room, and three-state views from a belvedere.

title: Fishing crew rescued near Gloucester after boat runs aground amid harsh seas
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two crew members rescued by Gloucester authorities after their fishing boat ran aground near Atlantic Road during rough seas.
The US Coast Guard and local responders attempted to free the boat amid 2 to 4-foot waves causing hull damage.
Rescue efforts involved crew members using survival suits and lines to reach safety; both were uninjured.

title: The Rafael Devers trade is already haunting the Red Sox in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox are struggling after trading Rafael Devers to the Giants, with a 2-7 start this season.
Boston's return from the Devers trade has been disappointing; of four players acquired, only Jose Bello remains.
Key acquisition Ranger Suarez has an 8.64 ERA in two starts, adding to the team's early-season challenges.

title: Why it might be time for a slumping Bruins team to call up James Hagens
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins have scored only three goals in their last three games, resulting in a three-game losing streak.
James Hagens, currently with the Providence Bruins, could potentially bolster Boston's struggling offense ahead of the playoffs.
Hagens posted 47 points in 34 games at Boston College and has shown promising skills in the AHL with four points in six games.

title: Tatum, Celtics pull away late to beat Raptors, move closer to 2-seed: 6 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown led the Celtics to a 113-95 victory over the Raptors, boosting their chance for the 2-seed.
Neemias Queta had a standout performance with 18 points on 9-for-10 shooting, seven rebounds, four assists, and three blocks.
Veteran center Nikola Vucevic returned from injury, contributing modestly with 2 points and needing time to adjust to the team.

title: New Bedford man gets decades-long prison sentence for raping young relative
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Daniel Turcios Torres, 46, was sentenced to 36-45 years in prison for raping a young relative.
Charges include multiple counts of rape, indecent assault on a child, and witness intimidation.
Turcios Torres allegedly threatened and intimidated the victim to prevent disclosure of the assaults.

title: She is running Boston in honor of her mother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Madison Oakes is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her mother who died of Stage 4 brain cancer.
Oakes credits Massachusetts General Hospital for extending her mother's life past an initial six months diagnosis.
Madison Oakes is a Boxford native and works as an oncology nurse, helping cancer patients in ICU-level care.

title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police fatally shot a man armed with a sword during a mental health crisis near Northeastern University.
The man attacked an officer, stabbing him in the arm, and a clinician before being shot by police.
The incident is under investigation by Boston police and the Suffolk County District Attorney’s Office.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois, 23 from Hudson, NH, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer.
Dubois qualified for the Boston Marathon after running the Lake Erie Marathon in September 2025.
She aims to raise funds for the Cancer Care Equity program at Massachusetts General Hospital through her marathon participation.
